hypotheses.text: A hostile caller can cause a traversal or unauthorized file read through the parameter-to-open() pattern in verdict/bundle.py.
Malformed hostile JSON can cause an opaque production failure at the bundle loader.

deductions.text: Neither observed path-to-open pattern proves that its parameter originates from an untrusted public interface; detector output itself records controllability as UNDETERMINED.
The unavailable package context prevents executed induction from connecting malformed JSON to the cited parser call and its externally visible effect.

work_product.decision: ABSTAIN: Do not promote either external candidate. Both remain UNDETERMINED because this review has source observations but no executed induction establishing reachable hostile control and a concrete mechanism.

adi.statement: The two parameter-to-open() observations could indicate traversal if an attacker controls the path values.
An uncaught json.load at the bundle loader could turn malformed JSON into an opaque operational failure.

A confirmed defect would require evidence that a public or otherwise hostile input reaches either parameter, bypasses any relevant normalization or authorization, and reads a target outside the intended boundary.
A confirmed defect would require a reproducible malformed externally supplied bundle that reaches line 96, produces the asserted failure, and lacks an intended higher-layer error boundary.
